Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to analyze a provided event log for a Procure to Pay P2P process I need to identify anomalies unusual sequences and explain why they are problematic based on standard business practices

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Standard P2P Process Standard Flow Create Purchase Requisition Approve Purchase Requisition Issue Purchase Order Receive Goods Vendor Invoice Received Match Invoice to PO Release Payment Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key Controls Logic Approval must happen before issuing a PO PO must usually exist before receiving goods Goods must be received before paying Invoice matching usually happens before payment release Payment is the final step before closing

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 Assessment This looks perfect Standard sequence logical timing Result No anomalies

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Flow Create PR Issue PO Wait where is the approval Invoice Received Release Payment paid already Approve PR Approval happening days after PO issue and even after payment Receive Goods Goods received after payment Match Invoice

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002 Anomaly 1 Issue Purchase Order happens before Approve Purchase Requisition This is a control failure unauthorized spend

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002 Anomaly 2 Release Payment happens before Receive Goods and before Match Invoice to PO This is a major risk paying for undelivered goods

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002 Anomaly 3 Approve Purchase Requisition happens late Approving a requisition after the PO is issued and the payment is made defeats the purpose of approval

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002 Anomaly 4 Receive Goods happens after payment

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Flow Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Standard start Release Payment Vendor Invoice Received Receive Goods Match Invoice

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 Anomaly 1 Release Payment occurs before the Vendor Invoice is received before Goods are received and before Matching Paying without an invoice or goods is extremely irregular prepayment without verification

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 Anomaly 2 Sequence is completely inverted at the end Payment Invoice Goods Match Usually Goods and Invoice trigger Match which triggers Payment

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Flow Create PR Approve PR Receive Goods Before PO Issue PO Retroactive PO Invoice Match Payment Close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004 Anomaly 1 Receive Goods happens slightly before Issue Purchase Order While the gap is small technically the PO should exist before goods are received This is often called Maverick Buying or Spot Buying where goods arrive before paperwork is finalized

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: However sometimes goods arrive early or the system entry is delayed

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Is it anomalous Yes it is a process deviation Is it a critical failure It implies the PO was created because goods arrived reactive rather than proactively

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize and Structure the Report Introduction state the standard P2P flow as the baseline Case by case analysis Case 1001 no issues Case 1002 ordering issues explain risks fraud paying for nothing Case 1003 pre payment issue explain risks cash flow fraud no verification Case 1004 goods before PO explain maverick buying or data entry timing issues

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Refining the Explanations Self Correction during drafting Case 1002 I need to highlight that Release Payment happened before Match Invoice This violates the 3 way match principle

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Case 1003 Payment happened before everything after the PO This suggests a potential advance payment or a data entry error but strictly speaking it is an anomaly

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Case 1004 The goods arrived 15 minutes before the PO In strict environments receiving should not be allowed without a PO number This is a process deviation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets double check Case 1002 timestamps Issue PO Release Payment Approve PR This is a mess Approval is after payment Payment is before goods receipt

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets double check Case 1003 timestamps Issue PO Release Payment Invoice Goods Payment is totally premature

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets double check Case 1004 timestamps Receive Goods Issue PO This is the Goods Receipt without PO anomaly

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction on Case 1004 Is it possible the PO was issued verbally and entered later Yes but in process mining event logs we judge by the recorded timestamps The recorded timestamp shows Receiving before PO This is a deviation
